data.title: Chunghwa Telecom reports Q1 results
data.description:
• Chunghwa Telecom press release (NYSE:CHT): Q1 Total revenue increased by 1.6% to NT$ 55.81 billion.
  • Consumer Business Group revenue decreased by 0.2% to NT$ 34.57 billion.
  • Enterprise Business Group revenue increased by 2.3% to NT$ 17.34 billion.
  • International Business Group revenue increased by 0.9% to NT$ 2.44 billion.
  • Total operating costs and expenses increased by 0.6% to NT$ 43.29 billion.
  • Operating income increased by 5.0% to NT$ 12.52 billion.
  • EBITDA increased by 3.2% to NT$ 22.53 billion.
  • Net income attributable to stockholders of the parent increased by 4.3% to NT$ 9.80 billion.
  • Basic earnings per share was NT$1.26.
  • Operating income, income before tax, net income attributable to stockholders of the parent and EPS all exceeded our proposed guidance.

data.title: Vor Bio plunges as it winds down all manufacturing operations, reduces 95% of workforce
data.description:
• Vor Bio (NASDAQ:VOR) said on Thursday that based on currently available clinical data from its key clinical programs and a challenging fundraising environment, the board has approved the initiation of a process to explore a range of strategic alternatives. 
  • These strategic alternatives could include, among other alternatives, a potential sale of assets of the company, a potential licensing of assets of the company, a sale of the company, a business combination, a merger or other strategic action.
  • The company is also winding down its clinical and manufacturing operations, beginning immediately, including its ongoing clinical trials. The decision to discontinue clinical operations is not due to any safety concerns with Vor Bio’s product candidates.
  • With the strategic process, Vor Bio implemented a workforce reduction of approximately 95% with a cost of approximately $10.9 million.
  • Vor Bio plans to retain approximately 8 employees to assist in exploring its strategic alternatives, and winding-down the clinical and manufacturing operations.
  • Vor Bio (NASDAQ:VOR) shares were down more than 44% in premarket trading.

data.title: Kiwetinohk Energy GAAP EPS of $1.23; updates FY25 guidance
data.description:
• Kiwetinohk Energy press release (TSX:KEC:CA): Q1 GAAP EPS of $1.23.
  • Record quarterly production of 32,611 boe/d, increased 18% over the fourth quarter (53% natural gas + 47% condensate and NGLs) with three new Duvernay wells and one Simonette Montney well brought on stream.
  • Generated $29.5 million in free cash flow3 on capital expenditures (before acquisitions/dispositions)3 of $86.4 million. Free cash flow supports the capital development program and debt repayments.
  • Reduced net debt to annualized adjusted funds flow from operations ratio4 by 25% from year end 2024 levels, exiting the quarter with a ratio of 0.75x.

  • Guidance update

    Kiwetinohk has reduced annual guidance for operating costs and transportation costs as a result of strong performance during the first quarter of 2025.

    • Projected operating costs have been reduced by $0.50/boe to a revised annual target range of $6.75 - $7.25/boe.
    • Projected transportation costs have been reduced by $0.25/boe to a revised annual target range of $5.75 - $6.00/boe.

data.title: First Advantage Non-GAAP EPS of $0.17 beats by $0.04, revenue of $354.6M beats by $10.36M
data.description:
• First Advantage press release (NASDAQ:FA): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.17 beats by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $354.6M (+109.3% Y/Y) beats by $10.36M.
  • Shares -9.49% PM.

  • Reaffirming Full Year 2025 Guidance

    • Reaffirming full year 2025 guidance ranges, including the expected benefits of realized synergies, for Revenues of $1.5 billion to $1.6 billion, Adjusted EBITDA of $410 million to $450 million, Adjusted Net Income of $152 million to $182 million, and Adjusted Diluted Earnings Per Share of $0.86 to $1.03
  • FY EPS consensus of $0.90
  • FY revenue consensus of $1.51B

data.title: Canadian Utilities appoints CEO, executive chair
data.description:
• Canadian Utilities Limited (TSX:CU:CA) has appointed Bob Myles as the chief executive officer and Nancy Southern as the executive chair of the company.
  • Both changes are effective May 8, 2025. 
  • Myles previously held the role of president and chief operating officer, Canadian Utilities.
  • Southern was first named chief executive officer of Canadian Utilities in 2000.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: BitFuFu produces 209 BTC in April
data.description:
• BitFuFu (NASDAQ:FUFU) announced 1,908 bitcoin (BTC) held in April, an increase of 61 BTC from March 31, 2025, attributed to self-mining operations and net Bitcoin inflow from customer payments.
  • 209 BTC produced in April 2025, including 173 BTC from cloud mining and 36 BTC from self-mining.
  • Total hashrate under management reached 28.3 EH/s as of April 30, 2025, a 37.4% month-over-month increase.
  • Total power capacity under management rose to 566 MW across five continents as of April 30, 2025, an 18.4% month-over-month increase.
  • FUFU shares up 2.9% premarket on Thursday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Salesforce commits $500M to AI growth in Saudi Arabia
data.description:
• Salesforce (NYSE:CRM) has announced plans to invest $500M over the next five years to boost artificial intelligence adoption in Saudi Arabia.
  • As part of the initiative, the U.S. tech firm is establishing a regional headquarters in Riyadh and aims to hire up to 40 employees by the end of the year.
  • The investment will support local recruitment, training programs, and other regional initiatives.
  • Salesforce has appointed former SAP (SAP) (OTCPK:SAPGF) executive Ibrahim Alseghayr as regional vice president and managing director for Saudi Arabia.
  • The move aligns with Saudi Arabia’s broader $100 billion AI strategy, which includes major investments in data centers and cloud infrastructure.
  • CRM shares up 1.2% premarket on Thursday. 
  • Source: Bloomberg report

data.title: Instacart CEO joins OpenAI's executive team
data.description:
Instacart’s (NASDAQ:CART) chief executive Fidji Simo is joining the OpenAI executive team as head of Applications, transitioning from the helm of the grocery delivery company to OpenAI over the next few months.

“Applications brings together a group of existing business and operational teams responsible for how our research reaches and benefits the world, and Fidji is uniquely qualified to lead this group. Serving as an OpenAI board member over the past year, Fidji has already contributed a great deal to our company,” OpenAI CEO Sam Altman said in a blog post on Wednesday.

By passing Applications responsibilities to Simo, Altman said he can now increase his focus on research, compute, and safety systems for the ChatGPT platform, ensuring the development of a “safe superintelligence infrastructure.”

During her 5 years at Instacart (NASDAQ:CART) Simo orchestrated a $660M initial public offering in 2023, founded Instacart Health, launched new apps including Fizz, and integrated Instacart with other platforms like Partiful. Following the COVID boom in home delivery, Simo led Instacart (NASDAQ:CART) to sales of nearly $1B and share appreciation of 52% since the company’s IPO.

Prior to Instacart (CART) Simo was at Meta (META) for 10 years as head of the Facebook app.

In a letter to Instacart (CART) employees, Simo said she will remain as chair of the company’s board to support the transition of her successor.

Instacart (CART) shares are lower on the news.
